news.summary: The Colombian company, Ecopetrol, revealed on June 27, 2025, that they have extended an agreement with JPMorgan Chase Bank N.A., the depositary bank for their ADR program. This agreement involves a reduction of 50% in conversion fees for the issuance.
news.headline: Ecopetrol extends 50% fee reduction for ADR conversion in the U.S. with JPMorgan Chase Bank N.A.

The meeting, as detailed on their official website www.integraresources.com, revealed significant shareholder participation and support for the company's endeavours. The exact numbers and details can be found on their platform.
news.headline: Integra Resources Corp reports voting results from AGM on June 27, 2025. Listed on TSXV: ITR and NYSE American: ITRG.

news.summary: In Chicago, The AZEK Company Inc. (NYSE: AZEK), a top manufacturer of environmentally sustainable outdoor living products, has received stockholder approval for its transaction with James Hardie Industries plc. The approved proposals relate to the acquisition and include the company's popular TimberTech® Decking and Railing, AZEK® and Versatex® Trim, and StruXure® pergolas. This development was confirmed at the Special Meeting of Stockholders, indicating the successful completion of this significant business deal.
news.headline: AZEK stockholders approve transaction with James Hardie. Company: (NYSE: AZEK), manufacturer of outdoor products.

news.summary: Richmond, Virginia-based real estate investment trust Medalist Diversified REIT (NASDAQ: MDRR) recently announced a quarterly dividend on its common stock at $0.0675 per share. This dividend declaration was made by the company's Board of Directors and is payable in cash. Medalist specializes in acquiring, owning, and managing commercial real estate in the Southeast region of the U.S.
news.headline: Medalist Diversified REIT, Inc. declares $0.0675 dividend per share on its common stock in Richmond, VA.

news.summary: In Washington, Citizens Against Government Waste (CAGW) has identified Senators Alex Padilla and Adam Schiff, both Democrats from California, as the June 2025 Porkers of the Month. They were selected for advocating to persist in squandering billions of taxpayer funds on California’s unsuccessful high-speed rail initiative. The Trump administration halted $4 billion for the California High-Speed Rail Authority (CHSRA) on June 4, 2025. The CHSRA's high-speed rail project, approved in 2008, has been derailed and has resulted in wasteful spending of taxpayer money.Character count: 509
news.headline: Citizens Against Government Waste labels Senators Alex Padilla and Adam Schiff Porkers of the Month for wanting to continue funding California's failed high-speed rail project; $4 billion pulled by Trump admin on June 4, 2025.

news.summary: Chemed Corporation (NYSE: CHE) based in Cincinnati revealed that the financial results for the second quarter ending June 30, 2025, will be disclosed on Tuesday, July 29, 2025, subsequent to the conclusion of trading on the New York Stock Exchange. The company also unveiled updates regarding second-quarter earnings and the full-year 2025 guidance. Earlier statements from the management hinted at the likelihood of VITAS subsidiary encountering an overall Medicare Cap billing constraint.
news.headline: Chemed (NYSE: CHE) to release Q2 earnings on July 29, 2025. Subsidiary VITAS may face Medicare Cap billing limitation.

news.summary: In Toronto, the Ontario Compensation Employees Union (OCEU/CUPE 1750) is thankful to the Ontario Nurses’ Association (ONA) for their backing of over 3,600 striking OCEU members. Through a letter to WSIB President and CEO Jeffery Lang on June 23, ONA urged WSIB management to resolve the strike by discussing a just deal handling workload, mental health, job security, and pay upgrades.
news.headline: OCEU/CUPE 1750 thanks ONA for supporting 3,600 striking members. ONA urges WSIB to negotiate fair agreement.

news.summary: Goldman Sachs analysts report that subprime consumers, typically those with credit scores below 660, are displaying resilience and spending power despite economic challenges such as tariffs and inflation. They predict positive spending growth and improving delinquency trends among this group. Consumer wealth in the lowest income quintile experienced a 7% increase year-over-year by May 2025. Unemployment rates were at 4.2% in May, with debt payments as a percentage of disposable income below the long-term average. Household debt as a share of GDP was at its lowest level since 1999, indicating financial stability among consumers. Subprime borrowers are adjusting their financial habits by cutting back on high-cost items. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Goldman Sachs sees subprime consumers as a lucrative market despite economic challenges, citing positive spending growth and improving delinquency trends. US Subprime wealth increased by 7% and unemployment stands at 4.2%, while debt payment is at 11.3%. Subprime borrowers are adapting financially and being cautious with expenditure.

news.summary: On June 27, 2025, NIQ Global Intelligence plc revealed its submission of a registration statement on Form S-1 to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for an upcoming initial public offering of its ordinary shares. The company plans to offer an undisclosed number of shares to the public, making it a significant financial move for NIQ Global Intelligence plc.
news.headline: NIQ files Form S-1 with SEC for IPO of ordinary shares - generating buzz in the market.

news.summary: United Community Banks, Inc. (NYSE: UCB) based in Greenville, S.C., has revealed plans to disclose its financial performance for the second quarter of 2025 on Wednesday, July 23, 2025, ahead of the stock market's opening. The company will host a conference call on the same day at 9:00 a.m. EST to elaborate on its financial results, business achievements, and future prospects.
news.headline: United Community Banks will release its Q2 2025 financial results on July 23, 2025, before the market opens, followed by a conference call at 9:00 a.m. EST on the same day.

news.summary: In Arlington, Virginia on June 27, 2025, RTX (NYSE: RTX) revealed that its Board of Directors has approved a dividend of 68 cents per share for its common stockholders. This dividend is set to be distributed on September 4, 2025, to shareholders on record as of the end of business on...
news.headline: RTX Board announced quarterly dividend of 68 cents per share payable on Sept 4, 2025 to shareholders of record.

news.summary: On June 27, 2025, Vitalhub Corp. disclosed the outcomes of its annual general meeting held online. The Company (TSX:VHI) (OTCQX:VHIBF) revealed that all director candidates were successfully voted onto the board, and MNP LLP was selected as auditors. These decisions were in alignment with the details outlined in the Company's management information circular released on May 8, 2025.
news.headline: VitalHub Corp. (TSX:VHI) reports election of all director nominees and appointment of MNP LLP as auditors at virtual AGM.

news.summary: Hapbee has recently appointed three new members to its board, bringing in senior leaders with expertise in the fields of technology, legal, and finance. This decision comes as the company prepares to enter the next phase of its growth. Additionally, Hapbee has announced the revocation of a Management Cease Trade Order (MCTO). This development marks a significant milestone for the company as it continues to strengthen its leadership team and move forward with its strategic plans.
news.headline: Ahsan Ashraf joins Jaylen Brown as Hapbee investor, executive, and board member, as Hapbee expands board by three members. Revocation of MCTO.

news.summary: On June 27, 2025, E-L Financial Corporation Limited (TSX:ELF) disclosed its initiation of a normal course issuer bid (the “Bid”), with plans to engage in the repurchase of its common shares (the “Shares”). To facilitate this, the company has established an automatic share purchase plan (“ASPP”) with its designated broker. The ASPP has secured approval from the Toronto Stock Exchange (the “Exchange”) and will be in operation starting June 30, 2025. The Company operates under various stock symbols, including TSX:ELF.PR.F, TSX:ELF.PR.G, and TSX:ELF.PR.H.
news.headline: E-L Financial Corp Limited sets up ASPP with designated broker to buy back common shares; plan effective from June 30, 2025.

news.summary: Quantum BioPharma Ltd. is set to finalize a non-brokered private placement of class A multiple voting shares at $50 per share, aiming to raise up to $600 in total. The biopharmaceutical company, listed on NASDAQ as QNTM, CSE as QNTM, and FRA as 0K91, focuses on developing innovative assets and biotech solutions. This Offering, which is anticipated to be fully taken up by current MVS holders, reflects their commitment to expanding the company's portfolio.
news.headline: Quantum BioPharma is announcing a private placement of class A multiple voting shares at $50 each, aiming for up to $600 aggregate gross proceeds.

news.summary: In Austin, Texas, on June 27, 2025, Home Trends & Design excitedly revealed the debut of its revamped website, providing customers with an improved online experience. The new platform, now live, presents a range of features to enhance user control, flexibility, and usability.
news.headline: Home Trends & Design launches revamped website for enhanced user experience with more control and flexibility.

news.summary: A recent report reveals that the economy grew by 4.3% in the last quarter, beating analysts' expectations. This marks the fastest pace of growth seen in the past five years. The increase was primarily driven by a surge in consumer spending, which rose by 6.8% during the same period. Additionally, business investment grew by 3.2%, with the manufacturing sector showing substantial improvement. Unemployment also saw a decline, dropping to 3.9%, the lowest rate recorded in a decade. These positive economic indicators have led to a boost in the stock market, with the main index reaching a record high of 35,000 points. Overall, the report suggests a strong and resilient economy, signaling promising prospects for the future.
news.headline: Bruckheimer puts egos aside to release 'F1' in theaters. #Hollywood #movieindustry

news.summary: Some Democratic politicians have stated that they will oppose crypto regulations unless concerns regarding potential conflicts of interest are resolved.
news.headline: Trump avoids addressing divesting in cryptocurrency as Dems require resolution of conflicts of interest to pass regulations.

news.headline: Bitcoin treasury giant Strategy faces numerous copycat lawsuits; Coinbase closes at all-time high.

news.summary: The Department of Health reported an increase in COVID-19 cases with over 3,000 new infections confirmed within the past 24 hours. This surge brings the total number of active cases to 20,000. In addition, there were 50 new fatalities reported, raising the total death toll to 500. Local hospitals are struggling to accommodate the rising number of patients, with 80% of ICU beds currently occupied. Authorities are urging the public to strictly adhere to health protocols to curb the spread of the virus.
news.headline: Learn about F1's production with producer Jerry Bruckheimer. More details on Bloomberg's website.

news.summary: In a latest development, it has been reported that the number of confirmed COVID-19 cases has surged to over 5,000, with the death toll climbing to more than 200. The government has implemented strict lockdown measures in an effort to curb the spread of the virus, with businesses and schools being shut down indefinitely. Health officials are urging the public to practice social distancing and proper hygiene to prevent further transmission of the disease. The healthcare system is being overwhelmed by the influx of patients, prompting authorities to take urgent action to increase hospital capacity and obtain necessary medical supplies. The situation remains critical, with experts warning that the numbers are expected to rise in the coming days.
news.headline: Stock market resilient, poised for more gains despite doubts.

news.summary: Pennsylvania American Water, based in Mechanicsburg, has revealed a new agreement to purchase the City of Pittston's wastewater collection system for $26.4 million. This system caters to about 4,400 customers within the city. Pennsylvania American Water, which already handles the drinking water service for Pittston, boasts over 135,000 customer connections in the wider region. The company aims to upkeep its dedication to customers by delivering top-notch water and wastewater services.
news.headline: Pennsylvania American Water to acquire Pittston's wastewater system for $26.4 million, serving 4,400 customers. 135,000 total connections in region.

news.summary: Unisys Corporation, based in Blue Bell, Pennsylvania, disclosed on June 27, 2025, the finalization of its recent offering of $700.0 million in total principal value of 10.625% Senior Secured Notes set to mature in 2031. The company, identified on the NYSE as UIS, finalised the transaction.
news.headline: Unisys closes $700M private offering of Senior Secured Notes; to refinance existing debt and fund pension plan.

news.summary: Select Medical Holdings Corporation, based in Mechanicsburg, Pennsylvania, is set to announce its financial results for the second quarter of 2025 on July 31, 2025, after the market closes. The company, listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol SEM, will conduct a conference following the release.
news.headline: Select Medical Holdings Corp to report Q2 2025 results on July 31, 2025 - after market closes. Trading as NYSE: SEM.
